meaning: n.喀拉喀托火山(印度尼西亚一火山岛) 喀拉喀托火山[印度尼西亚西南部](即腊卡塔火山)
examples: Coincidentally, the masterpiece was inspired by the powerful volcanic eruption of Krakatoa in 1883.
Krakatoa itself was virtually destroyed, although later eruptions have created a new island in the same place.
Some of the most violent past eruptions, like Krakatoa in Java, resulted from magma coming in contact with water.
The winter from 1883 -1884 KRAKATOA you will think was a little pebble, and now the stone comes.
It's known as Anak Krakatoa, or "child of Krakatoa, " and one day it too is likely to blow its top.
